title: Research MeshCore app transport/API protocol subset
description: Why this exists: the adapter must be based on what MeshCore app clients and firmware actually speak locally, not assumptions from Meshtastic or LICHEN Native. What needs to be done: inspect MeshCore app/client and firmware sources for local transport choices, framing, binary/protobuf/JSON formats if any, BLE/serial behavior, pairing/session model, node identity/status/config/message flows, and unsupported operations. Acceptance criteria: source references and commit SHAs are recorded in Beads notes; mandatory vs optional app protocol elements are listed; differences from Meshtastic and LICHEN Native are explicit; any uncertainty creates follow-up Beads.
notes:
MeshCore local API research evidence, recorded 2026-07-01.

Source baselines inspected:
- meshcore-dev/MeshCore e8d3c53ba1ea863937081cd0caad759b832f3028: docs/companion_protocol.md; examples/companion_radio/MyMesh.cpp/MyMesh.h; src/helpers/BaseSerialInterface.h; src/helpers/ArduinoSerialInterface.cpp; src/helpers/nrf52/SerialBLEInterface.cpp; src/helpers/esp32/SerialBLEInterface.cpp; src/helpers/esp32/SerialWifiInterface.cpp; examples/companion_radio/main.cpp.
- meshcore-dev/meshcore-open e1b8d6ec97d6ccca0eee8122864092f3af3f2062: documentation/ble-protocol.md; lib/connector/meshcore_protocol.dart; lib/connector/meshcore_connector.dart; lib/connector/meshcore_uuids.dart; lib/services/usb_serial_frame_codec.dart.
- meshcore-dev/meshcore_py 5bac3573b51c4298062881885b6d15a994109076: src/meshcore/packets.py; reader.py; ble_cx.py; serial_cx.py; tcp_cx.py; commands/device.py; commands/contact.py; commands/messaging.py; meshcore.py.
- meshcore-dev/meshcore-cli 3ad12c07beaac21210ed9b4b04c1fe8438722ecb: REPEATER_COMMANDS.md.
- meshcore-dev/meshcore.js 306dadac1cdacfd071ab6899e5c84e7def7c4425: src/constants.js; connection/web_ble_connection.js; serial_connection.js; nodejs_serial_connection.js; tcp_connection.js; connection.js.

Transport/framing findings:
- BLE companion mode is Nordic UART Service, service UUID 6E400001-B5A3-F393-E0A9-E50E24DCCA9E, RX app-to-firmware 6E400002-B5A3-F393-E0A9-E50E24DCCA9E, TX firmware-to-app 6E400003-B5A3-F393-E0A9-E50E24DCCA9E. BLE payload is exactly the raw MeshCore command/response frame; no serial length prefix. App side requests MTU 185 where supported. Firmware BLE implementations require encrypted MITM/PIN behavior.
- USB/UART serial and optional ESP32 TCP reuse the same raw MeshCore command payload with an outer 3-byte frame: app-to-device start 0x3c ('<') + uint16_le payload length + payload; device-to-app start 0x3e ('>') + uint16_le payload length + payload. TCP exists only on Wi-Fi ESP32 builds and defaults to port 5000.
- Inner frame format is one byte packet/command/response type plus variable payload. Multi-byte integers are little-endian. Strings are UTF-8 or fixed/null-padded fields. Firmware MAX_FRAME_SIZE is 176, while app docs/code use max payload 172 for serial/TCP-facing payload limits.
- MeshCore local compatibility is not protobuf, not JSON, not Meshtastic app framing, and not LICHEN IPv6/CoAP/SLIP. It is a byte-command companion protocol over NUS, serial, or TCP.

Session/startup findings:
- Recommended flow is connect/open transport, subscribe to BLE TX notifications if BLE, then serialize one command at a time and wait for the response/timeout while also accepting async push frames.
- Startup commands observed in docs/apps: CMD_APP_START (0x01) -> SELF_INFO (0x05); CMD_DEVICE_QUERY (0x16, app protocol version byte) -> DEVICE_INFO (0x0d); CMD_SET_DEVICE_TIME (0x06 + u32_le epoch) -> OK/ERR; CMD_GET_CONTACTS (0x04 + optional u32_le since) -> CONTACTS_START/CONTACT/CONTACT_END; CMD_GET_CHANNEL (0x1f + index) -> CHANNEL_INFO; CMD_SYNC_NEXT_MESSAGE (0x0a) until NO_MORE_MESSAGES, and repeat when MSG_WAITING push 0x83 arrives. Apps also query battery/storage, custom vars, auto-add config, and channel slots during richer sync.

Mandatory MeshCore-compat subset for LICHEN MVP:
- Transport surface: exact NUS UUIDs for BLE if BLE is exposed; raw binary BLE values; serial/TCP 0x3c/0x3e + uint16_le length if serial/TCP is exposed; bounded frame length; one in-flight command discipline; async push queue.
- Commands/responses: APP_START/SELF_INFO, DEVICE_QUERY/DEVICE_INFO, GET_CONTACTS with empty or synthesized contact list, GET_CHANNEL with deterministic channel slots, SYNC_NEXT_MESSAGE/NO_MORE_MESSAGES plus MSG_WAITING push, GET_BATT_AND_STORAGE with explicit placeholder/capability fields, GET/SET_DEVICE_TIME, OK/ERR with MeshCore error bytes.
- Messaging minimum: SEND_CHANNEL_TXT_MSG for app channel text ingress and CHANNEL_MSG_RECV/MSG_WAITING/SYNC_NEXT_MESSAGE for incoming app-visible messages if messaging is claimed. Direct SEND_TXT_MSG should be either mapped through a defined LICHEN peer identity projection or return deterministic ERR until that projection exists.
- Unsupported commands must return deterministic RESP_CODE_ERR (or documented harmless no-op/read-only response), not hang or silently drop.

Optional/capability-gated subset:
- Contact CRUD/share/export/import; channel write/delete; channel data datagrams; radio params and TX power writes; stats; telemetry; auto-add; default flood scope; path hash mode; BLE PIN change; private-message/direct text; remote repeater/admin/login/status/binary/anon/control flows; signing/private-key import/export/factory-reset/reboot.

Unsupported for LICHEN MVP unless explicitly implemented later:
- MeshCore RF interoperability, raw MeshCore packet send, repeater forwarding, MeshCore path/routing semantics as RF behavior, KISS modem mode, serial repeater text CLI, remote repeater/admin flows, private-key import/export, device signing, factory reset, and radio mutation that bypasses normal LICHEN policy.

Differences from Meshtastic and LICHEN Native:
- MeshCore local API uses 32-byte node public keys and 6-byte prefixes rather than Meshtastic node_num or LICHEN IPv6 identities. LICHEN must define a deterministic compatibility identity projection before direct messaging/contact mapping is real.
- MeshCore app channel/contact models are app-local compatibility concepts; LICHEN should synthesize or persist compatibility state without claiming MeshCore OTA compatibility.
- MeshCore channel info exposes channel secrets in its local API. LICHEN must not leak native group/OSCORE material through this field; use a compatibility-local placeholder/secret policy.
- The protocol has no sequence numbers; command serialization and async push demux are required.

Uncertainties discovered and filed/updated separately:
- Firmware/docs/client behavior disagree or have drift for some commands, including SEND_CHANNEL_TXT_MSG response (firmware observed OK while docs/Flutter path expects MSG_SENT in some places) and meshcore_py missing newer command enums such as SEND_CHANNEL_DATA/raw packet.
- BLE security/PIN/coexistence policy needs explicit LICHEN design.
- Identity projection, channel-secret policy, config write persistence/read-only policy, unsupported remote-admin matrix, and real-client smoke testing remain downstream Beads.

No code was changed for this research task. Commands/tools used locally: bd show/list/update/create; gh repository search; shallow git clones under /tmp/lichen-meshcore-research; rg/sed/nl inspections of the source files above. No build was required for research-only acceptance.
Review evidence: two independent review subagents completed after the research note and follow-up Beads were created. Feynman the 2nd found no P0/P1/P2 closure blockers; confirmed the note meets acceptance criteria, source SHAs are recorded, mandatory/optional/unsupported elements are separated, differences from Meshtastic/LICHEN Native are explicit, and uncertainties are routed to project-LICHEN-9c4y.13/.14/.15/.16/.17/.11. Pascal the 2nd independently cross-checked /tmp/lichen-meshcore-research clones and found no factual errors in UUID/framing, command IDs/responses, mandatory/optional boundaries, unsupported behavior, or follow-up coverage. Residual risk is real-client behavior, already tracked by project-LICHEN-9c4y.11 and command drift by project-LICHEN-9c4y.16.
close_reason: Completed: MeshCore local transport/API research recorded with source SHAs, protocol subset classification, differences, uncertainty follow-ups, and two clean reviews.
